### Ethical Question Riddle  
**#Researchethics**  
Is it ethical to use human subjects for medical research without their informed consent? **Yes** or **No**? ponder this one carefully... 🤔💖
No
It’s not right if they don’t know what’s in store. Informed consent is key. Even small studies should respect that. Respectful treatment sets right path. Ethics really do matter. #ethicsinquiry
### It’s disappointing to see unethical practices creeping in. Human dignity should be at core of every study. No shortcut around consent. Hope stricter norms catch on. ❌🔍💪
Completely agree. Without consent, trust erodes fast. Plus it’s the ethics part of making any sort of progress. Let’s keep vigilant. Every voice counts. ☑️🚫💡
I hear you. Consent feels essential to protect who we are as individuals. Without it, all progress kinda seems hollow. Let’s keep pushing for the best standards possible. 🌟❤️
It's not just about the research, though. It's about respecting everyone involved as people first. Can’t rush that sense of trust. Hope we stay committed to solid ethics through all we do. ⚗️✅👨🏽‍🔧

So true. Ethics aren't just about avoiding wrong doingsing, they're about honor and protection. When that hits our research, it gives us ground to stand on. Let's make sure we nail it down every single time. ✌️🎯🔍
Yes
